Use the many tools ChatGPT has to offer.
Follow these instructions to get your Op:
Follow these instructions to get your OpenAI API Key:

1. Visit the following website: https://platform.openai.com/account/api-keys.
2. Once on the website, locate and click on the option to obtain your OpenAI API Key.

It is strongly recommended that you add your credit card information to your OpenAI account and upgrade to the paid plan **before** generating the API Key. This will help you prevent 429 errors.

Returns structured data from provided un: Returns structured data from provided unstructured text.
Classify whether the supplied text viola: Classify whether the supplied text violates OpenAI safety policies (harassment, hate, self-harm, sexual, violence, etc.).
Generate a vector embedding for the supp: Generate a vector embedding for the supplied text. Useful for semantic search, clustering and RAG pipelines.

Translate audio to text using whisper-1: Translate audio to text using whisper-1 model
Upload a file to OpenAI for use with Ass: Upload a file to OpenAI for use with Assistants, Vector Stores, Batch jobs, Fine-tuning, or Vision.
Return the list of files uploaded to the: Return the list of files uploaded to the connected OpenAI account, optionally filtered by purpose.
Delete a file previously uploaded to the: Delete a file previously uploaded to the connected OpenAI account.
Return the list of models available to t: Return the list of models available to the connected OpenAI account, optionally filtered by an ID substring.

Question: Question
The model which will generate the comple: The model which will generate the completion. Some models are suitable for natural language tasks, others specialize in code.
Controls randomness: Lowering results in: Controls randomness: Lowering results in less random completions. As the temperature approaches zero, the model will become deterministic and repetitive.
The maximum number of tokens to generate: The maximum number of tokens to generate. Requests can use up to 2,048 or 4,096 tokens shared between prompt and completion depending on the model. Don't set the value to maximum and leave some tokens for the input. (One token is roughly 4 characters for normal English text)
An alternative to sampling with temperat: An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.
Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive va: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim.
Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the mode's likelihood to talk about new topics.
A memory key that will keep the chat his: A memory key that will keep the chat history shared across runs and flows. Keep it empty to leave ChatGPT without memory of previous messages.
Array of roles to specify more accurate: Array of roles to specify more accurate response
The assistant which will generate the co: The assistant which will generate the completion.
A memory key that will keep the chat his: A memory key that will keep the chat history shared across runs and flows. Keep it empty to leave your assistant without memory of previous messages.
The image URL or file you want GPT's vis: The image URL or file you want GPT's vision to read.
What do you want ChatGPT to tell you abo: What do you want ChatGPT to tell you about the image?
Control how the model processes the imag: Control how the model processes the image and generates textual understanding.
The maximum number of tokens to generate: The maximum number of tokens to generate. Requests can use up to 2,048 or 4,096 tokens shared between prompt and completion, don't set the value to maximum and leave some tokens for the input. The exact limit varies by model. (One token is roughly 4 characters for normal English text)
Moderation model to use. omni-moderation: Moderation model to use. omni-moderation-latest also supports images.
The text to embed. To embed multiple str: The text to embed. To embed multiple strings in one call, provide a newline-separated list or use the Custom API Call action.
Number of dimensions for the embedding (: Number of dimensions for the embedding (only supported by text-embedding-3-* models).
A stable identifier for the end user — h: A stable identifier for the end user — helps OpenAI detect abuse.

The resolution to generate the image in.
The image to edit (PNG, JPEG, or WebP).
A description of the desired edits or th: A description of the desired edits or the new image.
Optional mask image. Transparent areas i: Optional mask image. Transparent areas indicate where to apply edits. Must match the input image dimensions.
The size of the generated image.
The text you want to hear.
The model which will generate the audio.
The speed of the audio. Minimum is 0.25: The speed of the audio. Minimum is 0.25 and maximum is 4.00.
The voice to generate the audio in.
The format you want the audio file in.
The name of the output audio file (witho: The name of the output audio file (without extension).
Language of the audio file the default i: Language of the audio file the default is en (English).
The intended use of the file. Each purpo: The intended use of the file. Each purpose has different allowed file types — see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/files/create.
Optional. By default the original filena: Optional. By default the original filename is used. Provide a value to override (must include the extension).
If supplied, only files with this purpos: If supplied, only files with this purpose are returned.
Maximum number of files to return (1-100: Maximum number of files to return (1-10000). Defaults to 10000.
The OpenAI file ID returned by Upload Fi: The OpenAI file ID returned by Upload File (starts with `file-`).
Optional case-insensitive substring used: Optional case-insensitive substring used to filter model IDs (e.g. `gpt-4`, `embedding`, `whisper`).
Authorization headers are injected autom: Authorization headers are injected automatically from your connection.
